perturbation.original_text: 6. Return/Destruction of Confidential Information. If either Party hereto determines not to proceed with the Transaction, it will immediately notify the other Party hereto in writing of that decision. Upon sending of such written notice or earlier upon the request of the Provider, all copies of Confidential Information provided to or in the possession of the Reviewer or its Representatives shall be destroyed or returned promptly to the Provider, together with (i) all copies thereof made by the Reviewer or its Representatives, and (ii) all portions of all compilations, studies, notes, analyses and memoranda prepared in connection with the examination thereof or derived therefrom that contain or reflect any Confidential Information. Upon request of the Provider, the Reviewer shall provide to the Provider a written certificate as to the return or destruction of such Confidential Information. Any oral Confidential Information will continue to be held subject to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Return/Destruction of Confidential Information. If either Party hereto determines not to proceed with the Transaction, it will immediately notify the other Party hereto in writing of that decision. Upon sending of such written notice or earlier upon the request of the Provider, all copies of Confidential Information provided to or in the possession of the Reviewer or its Representatives shall be destroyed or returned promptly to the Provider, together with (i) all copies thereof made by the Reviewer or its Representatives, and (ii) all portions of all compilations, studies, notes, analyses and memoranda prepared in connection with the examination thereof or derived therefrom that contain or reflect any Confidential Information. Upon request of the Provider, the Reviewer shall provide to the Provider a written certificate as to the return or destruction of such Confidential Information. Any oral Confidential Information does not need to be returned.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that oral Confidential Information does not need to be returned, which contradicts the previous sentence stating 'Any oral Confidential Information will continue to be held subject to this Agreement,' which implies that oral confidential information should also be returned or destroyed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Return/Destruction of Confidential Information. If either Party hereto determines not to proceed with the Transaction, it will immediately notify the other Party hereto in writing of that decision. Upon sending of such written notice or earlier upon the request of the Provider, all copies of Confidential Information provided to or in the possession of the Reviewer or its Representatives shall be destroyed or returned promptly to the Provider, together with (i) all copies thereof made by the Reviewer or its Representatives, and (ii) all portions of all compilations, studies, notes, analyses and memoranda prepared in connection with the examination thereof or derived therefrom that contain or reflect any Confidential Information. Upon request of the Provider, the Reviewer shall provide to the Provider a written certificate as to the return or destruction of such Confidential Information. Any oral Confidential Information does not need to be returned.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Any oral Confidential Information will continue to be held subject to this Agreement'. The changed text says 'Any oral Confidential Information does not need to be returned.' This creates a direct contradiction because the original implies oral information is subject to the agreement's return/destruction clause, while the changed text explicitly exempts it. This is an 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' according to the provided definition because different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Standstill; Non-Solicitation of Employees. (a) Buyer agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, neither Buyer nor any subsidiary or affiliate of Buyer will, without the prior written consent of CBL or its board of directors: (i) acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any voting securities or rights or options to acquire any voting securities, or securities or instruments convertible into voting securities, of CBL; (ii) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any "SOLICITATION" of "PROXIES" (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of CBL; (iii) make any public announcement in connection with any of the foregoing; (iv) form, join or participate in a "GROUP" (as defined in section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) in connection with any of the foregoing; or (v) otherwise attempt or join in an attempt by another person or entity that intends to attempt to gain control of CBL. In the event that Buyer becomes aware of any inadvertent acquisition of securities of CBL covered by the foregoing, Buyer shall immediately so notify CBL and as promptly as possible cause such securities to be divested in a commercially reasonable manner.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Standstill; Non-Solicitation of Employees. (a) Buyer agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, neither Buyer nor any subsidiary or affiliate of Buyer will, without the prior written consent of CBL or its board of directors: (i) acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any voting securities or rights or options to acquire any voting securities, or securities or instruments convertible into voting securities, of CBL; (ii) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any "SOLICITATION" of "PROXIES" (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of CBL; (iii) make any public announcement in connection with any of the foregoing; (iv) form, join or participate in a "GROUP" (as defined in section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) in connection with any of the foregoing; or (v) otherwise attempt or join in an attempt by another person or entity that intends to attempt to gain control of CBL. In the event that Buyer becomes aware of any inadvertent acquisition of securities of CBL covered by the foregoing, Buyer doesn't need to notify CBL.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, it states that if Buyer inadvertently acquires securities of CBL, they must notify CBL. The modified text then indicates that Buyer doesn't need to notify CBL, making it unclear if a notification is required. This contradicts the obligations set forth in the prior sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Standstill; Non-Solicitation of Employees. (a) Buyer agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, neither Buyer nor any subsidiary or affiliate of Buyer will, without the prior written consent of CBL or its board of directors: (i) acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any voting securities or rights or options to acquire any voting securities, or securities or instruments convertible into voting securities, of CBL; (ii) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any "SOLICITATION" of "PROXIES" (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of CBL; (iii) make any public announcement in connection with any of the foregoing; (iv) form, join or participate in a "GROUP" (as defined in section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) in connection with any of the foregoing; or (v) otherwise attempt or join in an attempt by another person or entity that intends to attempt to gain control of CBL. In the event that Buyer becomes aware of any inadvertent acquisition of securities of CBL covered by the foregoing, Buyer doesn't need to notify CBL.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by stating that the Buyer 'doesn't need to notify CBL' if they inadvertently acquire securities, while the original text stated they 'shall immediately so notify CBL'. Since the contradicted text and changed text are the same, there is a clear and direct conflict, as one requires notification and the other explicitly states notification is not required, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Breach of Agreement; Indemnity. (a) In the event the Reviewer or any of its Representatives fails in any respect to comply with its obligations under this Agreement, the Reviewer shall be liable to the Provider for breach of this Agreement. The Reviewer understands and agrees that monetary damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or contemplated breach of this Agreement, and that the Provider shall be entitled to specific performance or other equitable relief by way of restraining orders and injunctions as a remedy for any such breach or contemplated breach without proof of actual damages and without the necessity of posting any bond. (b) The rights, powers, and remedies provided for in Section 9(a) shall be in addition to and shall not preclude the exercise of any other right, power, or remedy available to the Provider at law or in equity. No forbearance, failure, or delay in exercising any such right, power, or remedy shall operate as a waiver thereof or preclude its further exercise. (c) The Reviewer hereby indemnifies and holds harmless the Provider from and against any and all claims, demands, losses, actions, debts, liabilities, judgements, costs and attorneys' fees and disbursements arising out of, claimed on account of, or in any matter predicted upon or contributed to by a breach of this Agreement by the Reviewer or its Representatives or otherwise incurred by the Provider in enforcing or preserving the Provider's rights under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Breach of Agreement; Indemnity. (a) In the event the Reviewer or any of its Representatives fails in any respect to comply with its obligations under this Agreement, the Reviewer shall be liable to the Provider for breach of this Agreement. The Reviewer understands and agrees that monetary damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or contemplated breach of this Agreement, and that the Provider shall be entitled to specific performance or other equitable relief by way of restraining orders and injunctions as a remedy for any such breach or contemplated breach without proof of actual damages and without the necessity of posting any bond. (b) The rights, powers, and remedies provided for in Section 9(a) shall be in addition to and shall not preclude the exercise of any other right, power, or remedy available to the Provider at law or in equity. No forbearance, failure, or delay in exercising any such right, power, or remedy shall operate as a waiver thereof or preclude its further exercise. (c) The Reviewer is not responsible for indemnifying or holding harmless the Provider from and against any and all claims, demands, losses, actions, debts, liabilities, judgements, costs and attorneys' fees and disbursements arising out of, claimed on account of, or in any matter predicted upon or contributed to by a breach of this Agreement by the Reviewer or its Representatives or otherwise incurred by the Provider in enforcing or preserving the Provider's rights under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction within Section 9. Initially, Section 9(c) states that the Reviewer indemnifies and holds harmless the Provider. The modified text in 9(c) then indicates that the Reviewer is not responsible for indemnifying or holding harmless the Provider, creating a direct contradiction regarding the indemnity obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Breach of Agreement; Indemnity. (a) In the event the Reviewer or any of its Representatives fails in any respect to comply with its obligations under this Agreement, the Reviewer shall be liable to the Provider for breach of this Agreement. The Reviewer understands and agrees that monetary damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or contemplated breach of this Agreement, and that the Provider shall be entitled to specific performance or other equitable relief by way of restraining orders and injunctions as a remedy for any such breach or contemplated breach without proof of actual damages and without the necessity of posting any bond. (b) The rights, powers, and remedies provided for in Section 9(a) shall be in addition to and shall not preclude the exercise of any other right, power, or remedy available to the Provider at law or in equity. No forbearance, failure, or delay in exercising any such right, power, or remedy shall operate as a waiver thereof or preclude its further exercise. (c) The Reviewer is not responsible for indemnifying or holding harmless the Provider from and against any and all claims, demands, losses, actions, debts, liabilities, judgements, costs and attorneys' fees and disbursements arising out of, claimed on account of, or in any matter predicted upon or contributed to by a breach of this Agreement by the Reviewer or its Representatives or otherwise incurred by the Provider in enforcing or preserving the Provider's rights under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 9(c) of the agreement. The original text of Section 9(c) states that 'The Reviewer hereby indemnifies and holds harmless the Provider,' while the changed text states 'The Reviewer is not responsible for indemnifying or holding harmless the Provider.' This directly contradicts the initial obligation, creating a strong in-text contradiction within Section 9, as the indemnity obligation is both affirmed and denied within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Term. Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement shall terminate two years after the date of the destruction or return of the Confidential Information to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Term. Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement shall terminate five years after the date of the destruction or return of the Confidential Information to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change affects the term of the agreement. The original text says the agreement terminates two years after the return or destruction of confidential information. Changing the term to five years creates a direct contradiction with Section 7 regarding the Standstill agreement, which is set for one to two years from the date of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
7. Standstill; Non-Solicitation of Employees. (a) Buyer agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, neither Buyer nor any subsidiary or affiliate of Buyer will, without the prior written consent of CBL or its board of directors: (i) acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any voting securities or rights or options to acquire any voting securities, or securities or instruments convertible into voting securities, of CBL; (ii) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any "SOLICITATION" of "PROXIES" (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of CBL; (iii) make any public announcement in connection with any of the foregoing; (iv) form, join or participate in a "GROUP" (as defined in section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) in connection with any of the foregoing; or (v) otherwise attempt or join in an attempt by another person or entity that intends to attempt to gain control of CBL. In the event that Buyer becomes aware of any inadvertent acquisition of securities of CBL covered by the foregoing, Buyer doesn't need to notify CBL.
(b) Buyer agrees that, for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, neither Buyer nor any subsidiary or affiliate of Buyer will solicit for employment any executive officer or senior employee of CBL; provided, however, (i) Buyer and Buyer's subsidiaries and affiliates shall not be prohibited from employing any such individual who contacts Buyer of any of Buyer's subsidiaries or affiliates on his or her own initiative, and (ii) the foregoing restrictions shall not apply to general solicitations to hire through the use of advertising. Upon Buyer's request, CBL shall provide Buyer with a list of the names and titles of the individuals covered by this Section 7(b).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement's termination date to five years after the destruction or return of confidential information. This directly contradicts Section 7(b), which limits the non-solicitation of employees to two years from the agreement date. As the term of the non-solicitation agreement would extend beyond the end of the agreement based on the Changed Text, this creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Binding Effect. This Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns; provided, however, that neither Party shall assign any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Binding Effect. This Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns; provided, however, that either Party can assign any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The change in section 11 introduces a contradiction regarding the assignability of rights and obligations. The original clause states that neither party can assign rights or obligations without the other party's consent. The change now indicates that either party can assign rights and obligations without consent. This creates uncertainty regarding the parties' freedom to transfer their contractual commitments, impacting the enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Binding Effect. This Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns; provided, however, that either Party can assign any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party can assign rights or obligations without the other party's consent. The changed text indicates that either party can assign rights and obligations without consent. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, regarding the parties' ability to transfer their contractual commitments. This contradiction aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections (in this case, the same section before and after the change) provide conflicting obligations, creating uncertainty about which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Entire Agreement. This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the Parties and relating to the subject matter hereof and to the treatment of Confidential Information heretofore or hereafter reviewed or inspected by either Party or its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Entire Agreement. This Agreement represents only a partial agreement between the Parties and relating to the subject matter hereof and to the treatment of Confidential Information heretofore or hereafter reviewed or inspected by either Party or its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The alteration to Section 16 regarding the 'Entire Agreement' clause now specifies that the document constitutes 'only a partial agreement.' This directly contradicts the usual function of an 'Entire Agreement' clause, which is to confirm that the written contract contains all the agreed terms and supersedes any prior agreements or discussions. By stating it's a partial agreement, it introduces an ambiguity, suggesting that there may be other binding agreements or understandings not included in the document. This creates an in-text contradiction with all the other clauses in the contract, because it casts doubt on whether those clauses fully define the parties' obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. Entire Agreement. This Agreement represents only a partial agreement between the Parties and relating to the subject matter hereof and to the treatment of Confidential Information heretofore or hereafter reviewed or inspected by either Party or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is the 'entire agreement,' implying it contains all agreed terms. The changed text modifies this to state it is 'only a partial agreement,' which directly contradicts the function of an entire agreement clause. This creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text as well, because both sections contain the language 'only a partial agreement.' The explanation provided elaborates on how this change causes a direct contradiction because it casts doubt on all the other clauses within the contract.
